---
title: "III. Jesus in Creation"
format:
  pdf:
    toc: false
    number-sections: false
    geometry: margin=1in
    fontsize: 11pt
    pdf-engine: xelatex
mainfont: "Arial"
---

# III. Jesus in Creation

[Opening Song](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=upu5Lk5t5QM&list=RDupu5Lk5t5QM&start_radio=1)


## Introduction — Why I Think This Matters

## A. Jesus in Creation from Scripture

### 1. Overview
 a. [Creation Video — Bible Project](https://bibleproject.com/videos/genesis-1/)


### 2. New Testament Passages
 a. [John 1:1-3](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+1%3A1-3&version=CSB) — The Word (Jesus) was with God in the beginning; all things were created through Him

 b. [Colossians 1:15-17](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Colossians+1%3A15-17&version=WEB) — Jesus is the image of the invisible God; all things created through Him and for Him; in Him all things hold together

 c. [Hebrews 1:1-3](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Hebrews+1%3A1-3&version=WEB) — God created the world through His Son, who upholds the universe by the word of His power

 d. [1 Corinthians 8:6](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=1Cor+8%3A6&version=WEB) — One Lord Jesus Christ, through whom are all things

 e. [Ephesians 3:9](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Eph+3%3A9&version=WEB) — God created all things (some manuscripts: "through Jesus Christ")


### 3. Old Testament Passages
 a. [Genesis 1-2:2](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Genesis%201-2%3A3&version=WEB) — "Let us make man in our image" — plural language hinting at plurality within God

 b. [Proverbs 8:22-31](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Proverbs+8%3A22-31&version=WEB) — Wisdom personified as present and active at creation (often interpreted as pointing to the pre-incarnate Christ)


## B. Scriptures On Six Day Event and Man Made on Day Six

### 1. Six-Day Creation

#### a. How long is a day?
  i. Twenty-four hours

  ii. Daytime (daylight hours)

  iii. An era or period of time

  iv. In my father's day, it could take three days to drive across Texas driving only during the day


#### b. Scripture on days

#### i. Six-Day Creation Scripture References
  • [Genesis 1:5–31](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Genesis+1%3A5-31&version=WEB) — "Morning and evening" — a day

  • [Exodus 20:8–11](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Exodus+20%3A8-11&version=WEB) — Direct statement: creation in six days

  • [Exodus 31:17](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Exodus+31%3A17&version=WEB) — Direct statement: creation in six days

  • [Hebrews 4:4](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Hebrews+4%3A4&version=WEB) — Reference to seventh-day rest

  • [Exodus 31:18](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Exodus+31%3A18&version=WEB) — "…the two tablets… written with the finger of God"

  • [Exodus 32:16](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Exodus+32%3A16&version=WEB) — "The tablets were the work of God, and the writing was the writing of God"

  • [Deuteronomy 9:10](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Deuteronomy+9%3A10&version=WEB) — "…the two tablets of stone written with the finger of God"


### 2. Man Created in the Beginning (Day 6)
 a. Popular view: man appeared billions of years after creation via evolution


#### b. Scripture on man existing from the beginning

#### i. New Testament Scriptures
  • [Mark 10:6](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Mark+10%3A6&version=WEB) — "From the beginning of creation, God made them male and female"

  • [Matthew 19:4](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Matthew+19%3A4&version=WEB) — "From the beginning God made them male and female"

  • [Luke 11:50–51](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Luke+11%3A50-51&version=WEB) — Abel referenced from the foundation of the world

  • [Acts 17:26](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Acts+17%3A26&version=WEB) — Humanity descended from one man

  • [1 Corinthians 15:45](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=1+Corinthians+15%3A45&version=WEB) — Adam identified as the first man

  • [Hebrews 4:3; 9:26](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Hebrews+4%3A3%3B+9%3A26&version=WEB) — Events tied to the foundation of the world

  • [Revelation 13:8](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Revelation+13%3A8&version=WEB) — Redemptive plan from the foundation of the world


#### ii. Old Testament Scriptures
  • [Genesis 1:26–27](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Genesis+1%3A26-27&version=WEB) — God made man on Day 6, in His image

  • [Genesis 2:7](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Genesis+2%3A7&version=WEB) — "the Lord God formed the man of dust from the ground"

  • [Genesis 5:1–2](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Genesis+5%3A1-2&version=WEB) — "When God created man, he made him in the likeness of God"


## C. God's Character — Did He Mislead Us?

### 1. God Cannot Lie or Deceive
 a. [Hebrews 6:18](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Hebrews+6%3A18&version=WEB) — "it is impossible for God to lie"

 b. [Titus 1:2](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Titus+1%3A2&version=WEB) — "God, who never lies, promised before the ages began"

 c. [Numbers 23:19](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Numbers+23%3A19&version=WEB) — "God is not man, that he should lie… Has he said, and will he not do it?"

 d. [Malachi 3:6](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Malachi+3%3A6&version=WEB) — "For I the Lord do not change"

 e. [Hebrews 13:8](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Hebrews+13%3A8&version=WEB) — "Jesus Christ is the same yesterday and today and forever"

 f. [James 1:17](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=James+1%3A17&version=WEB) — "with whom there is no variation or shadow due to change"

 g. [Deuteronomy 32:4](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Deuteronomy+32%3A4&version=WEB) — "A God of faithfulness and without iniquity, just and upright is he"

 h. [Romans 3:4](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Romans+3%3A4&version=WEB) — "Let God be true though every one were a liar"

 i. [1 Samuel 15:29](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=1+Samuel+15%3A29&version=WEB) — "the Glory of Israel will not lie or have regret"

 j. [2 Timothy 2:13](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=2+Timothy+2%3A13&version=WEB) — "he remains faithful — for he cannot deny himself"


### 2. God Did Not Use Death to Create

#### a. "Very Good" creation did not have death
  • [Genesis 1:31](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Genesis+1%3A31&version=WEB) — "God saw everything that he had made, and behold, it was very good"

  • Death and suffering are incompatible with a creation God declared "very good"


#### b. Death entered through the Fall — Man's Rebellion
  • [Genesis 2:17](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Genesis+2%3A17&version=WEB) — "in the day that you eat of it you shall surely die"

  • [Genesis 3:17–19](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Genesis+3%3A17-19&version=WEB) — curse on creation; "to dust you shall return"

  • [Romans 5:12](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Romans+5%3A12&version=WEB) — "sin came into the world through one man, and death through sin"

  • [Romans 6:23](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Romans+6%3A23&version=WEB) — "the wages of sin is death"

  • [1 Corinthians 15:21](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=1+Corinthians+15%3A21&version=WEB) — "by a man came death… by a man has come also the resurrection"


#### c. New Creation will have no death or suffering
  • [Revelation 21:3–4](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Revelation+21%3A3-4&version=WEB) — "He will wipe away every tear… death shall be no more, neither shall there be mourning, nor crying, nor pain anymore"


#### d. Death as enemy — not a creative tool
  • [1 Corinthians 15:26](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=1+Corinthians+15%3A26&version=WEB) — "The last enemy to be destroyed is death"


#### e. Jesus defeated death — He would not have designed creation through it
  • [John 11:25](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+11%3A25&version=WEB) — "I am the resurrection and the life"

  • [2 Timothy 1:10](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=2+Timothy+1%3A10&version=WEB) — Christ "abolished death and brought life and immortality to light"

  • [Hebrews 2:14](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Hebrews+2%3A14&version=WEB) — "through death he might destroy the one who has the power of death"

  • [Revelation 1:18](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Revelation+1%3A18&version=WEB) — "I have the keys of Death and Hades"


### 3. God and Jesus Spoke as Though the Stories Were True
 a. Jesus referenced the beginning and Noah's flood as historical events

  • [Matthew 19:4](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Matthew+19%3A4&version=WEB) — "Have you not read that he who created them from the beginning made them male and female"

  • [Matthew 24:37–38](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Matthew+24%3A37-38&version=WEB) — "As were the days of Noah, so will be the coming of the Son of Man"

  • [Luke 17:26–27](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Luke+17%3A26-27&version=WEB) — Noah's flood treated as literal historical event

 b. All mentions of creation and the flood carry no hint of allegory or fable

  • [Romans 5:12–14](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Romans+5%3A12-14&version=WEB) — Adam treated as a real historical person whose sin had real consequences

  • [1 Peter 3:20](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=1+Peter+3%3A20&version=WEB) — Noah's flood referenced as historical fact

  • [2 Peter 3:5–6](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=2+Peter+3%3A5-6&version=WEB) — Creation and flood both affirmed as real events


## D. Some Common Sense Readings of the Book of Nature

### 1. Creation of the Universe
 a. Time, matter, and space were all created together

  i. Einstein showed they are coupled and interdependent

  ii. E = MC² — mass and energy are interlinked

  iii. Therefore time, space, and mass/energy came into existence at the same moment

  iv. [Video: Time, Space, and Matter](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=w6AHcv19NIc)

 b. Whatever created the material world could not itself be material

  i. [Creator joke (clean version)](https://www.youtube.com/shorts/saeW0lqCufM)

 c. Second Law of Thermodynamics

  i. Things do not naturally go from disorder to order

  ii. Incredible complexity and order in the cosmos requires an explanation

  iii. Fine-tuning of physical constants points to intentional design


### 2. Creation of Life
 a. Life did not spring into existence from non-life

  i. A "simple" cell is not simple

  ii. Information always comes from a mind — DNA and RNA are coded with information


### 3. Early Christians All Believed in a Young Earth
 a. Church fathers took creation literally

  • Basil the Great, Ambrose, Ephrem the Syrian — all interpreted the creation days as literal 24-hour days

  • No allegorical reading of Genesis 1 among the early fathers was motivated by science; figurative readings (e.g., Augustine) were based on literary, not scientific, concerns

 b. Christian founders of modern science believed in a young earth

  • Kepler, Newton, Faraday, Linnaeus — held to a recent creation consistent with Scripture


## E. Physical Evidence Consistent with a Young Earth

### 1. Evidence for Noah's Flood and Rapid Geological Formations
 a. Ancient flood legends: Many cultures worldwide have flood legends remarkably similar to the account of Noah's Flood.

 b. Early civilizations: Historical records and civilizations appear suddenly around 3000–4000 BC with advanced knowledge, with no evidence of millions of years of gradual primitive development.

 c. Polystrate fossils: Trees and animal remains found vertically spanning multiple sedimentary layers supposedly deposited over millions of years.

  i. [Example image](jesus-in-creation_files/Polystrate.png)

 d. Fossil graveyards: Massive concentrations of rapidly buried creatures (e.g., dinosaur bone beds, fish shoals).

 e. Soft tissue in dinosaur fossils: Original proteins, blood vessels, and cells in bones dated to 65+ million years old (e.g., Mary Schweitzer's T. rex finds).

 f. Rapid canyon formation: Canyons (e.g., Mount St. Helens 1980 eruption) forming in days or weeks, not millions of years.

 g. Flat gaps / paraconformities: Missing supposed millions of years with little to no erosion between sedimentary layers. [Video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Iqq-wPyEvoE&t=1s)

 h. Great Missoula Flood: [Long video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=x6-W0eDsE3I) | [Lake Missoula flood — Creationist interpretation](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gBvNVaPYmy8)

 i. Bent rock layers without fracturing: Sedimentary strata tightly folded without the heat/pressure needed to deform hardened rock — suggesting layers were still soft when folded. [Example image](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/5/55/Folding_of_alternate_layers_of_limestone_layers_with_chert_layers.jpg)


### 2. Radiometric Dating Issues
 a. Inconsistent dates: Different radiometric methods applied to the same rock often yield wildly different ages.

 b. Excess helium in zircons: High helium levels from uranium decay suggest only thousands of years of decay rather than billions (RATE project).

 c. Carbon-14 in "ancient" materials: Detectable C-14 in diamonds, coal, and fossils claimed to be millions of years old (C-14 half-life ~5,730 years limits detection to <100,000 years).

 d. Accelerated decay evidence: Proposals of rapid nuclear decay during the creation week or the Flood.


### 3. Biological and Genetic Evidence
 a. Human population growth: Current growth rates extrapolated backward fit a starting population of ~8 people (Noah's family) ~4,500 years ago.

 b. Genetic entropy / mutation load: Accumulation of harmful mutations suggests genomes are degrading rapidly, inconsistent with deep time (John Sanford).

 c. Living fossils: Species (e.g., coelacanth, horseshoe crab) essentially unchanged from fossils claimed to be hundreds of millions of years old.

 d. Lack of transitional forms: Ongoing controversy over clear evolutionary intermediates in the fossil record.


### 4. Astronomical and Cosmological Arguments
 a. Comet lifetimes: Short-period comets lose material quickly and should not exist after millions of years without a constant source (Oort cloud hypothesized but unobserved).

 b. Magnetic field decay: Earth's magnetic field is decaying at a measurable rate; extrapolating backward, it would have been impossibly strong only tens of thousands of years ago.

 c. Spiral galaxy arms: Galaxies should wind up tightly after a few hundred million years, yet many retain clear spiral structure.

 d. Moon recession: The Moon is moving away from Earth; backward extrapolation suggests catastrophically close proximity within ~1–2 billion years (not 4.5 billion).


### 5. Salt in the Ocean
 a. Sodium input into the oceans from rivers exceeds removal rates. At current rates, the oceans should be much saltier if Earth were billions of years old.


### 6. Sediment and Erosion Rates
 a. Continents would erode down to sea level in roughly 10–20 million years at current erosion rates.

 b. Ocean sediment thickness is far less than expected for hundreds of millions of years of accumulation.
